request.question: What MTV television drama starred an actor who had once boxed with a boxer born in Belarus?

title: James Newman (actor)
Yuri Foreman
Jada (band)
Andrew Matarazzo
Ball Trap on the Cote Sauvage
Zay Harding
The Lyon's Den
Charlie Carver
Ron Mustafaa
Aly & AJ: Sister Act
sentences: James Milo Newman (born Jared Matthew James Newman; January 22, 1992) is an American actor.
He played Tony Schneider on the MTV television teen drama "Skins".
Newman's first acting experience was auditioning for "Skins".
He had previously planned to fight in the Golden Gloves boxing tournament (he had once boxed with Yuri Foreman).
Yuri Foreman (born August 5, 1980) is an Israeli professional boxer who held the WBA super welterweight title from 2009 to 2010.
He was born in Gomel, Belarus, but currently fights out of Brooklyn, New York.
Jada is an American pop and R&B female vocal group from Boston, Massachusetts.
They are known for their mix of pop, soul, dance, and R&B styling, and their gospel-inspired harmonies.
Jada was signed by Universal Motown Records president Sylvia Rhone in October 2006.
Jada won “Outstanding Pop/R&B Music Act of the Year” at the 2008 Boston Music Awards.
Jada is managed by Laura Poulin Management/ Bristol Entertainment.
Through Universal Motown, Jada released their first major label single, "American Cowboy" (written and produced by Akon and RedOne) in the spring of 2009.
This was followed by their second major label single, "Break Up Song" (written and produced by Wayne Wilkins, Andrew Frampton, and Savan Kotecha) in the fall of 2009.
Jada’s song "Model That" was featured on the November 17, 2009 episode of the MTV television show "The City".
"American Cowboy" was featured on the Fox drama "House" on Monday, April 27, 2009.
They released a song and music video for "This Party’s on Fire" (written and produced by Rio, video directed by Vassili Shields) on October 4, 2011.

Their most recent EP, named "Supersonique", was released on January 24, 2011, and included 5 songs with 2 remixes, including "Supersonique", "This Party's on Fire", "Mercy Killing", "Can't Let Go", and "Goodbye".
Andrew Matarazzo (born March 4, 1997) is a Los Angeles-based actor best known for joining the cast of the MTV television series "Teen Wolf" in its final season.

Ball Trap on the Cote Sauvage is a 1989 British television comedy drama (transmitted on BBC1), written by celebrated screenwriter Andrew Davies and directed by Jack Gold, set in France on Brittany's Côte Sauvage.
The one-off drama starred Jack Shepherd, Zoë Wanamaker, Michael Kitchen, Miranda Richardson and Erika Hoffman

Zavan Kerr "Zay" Harding (born November 1, 1974, Lihue, Hawaii) is an American television personality and actor, who may be best known as "Jeff Northcutt" on the MTV television serial, "Spyder Games", and as a host of the adventure travel television series, "Globe Trekker".
He is also the host of "Tough Trains", a travel series from the producers of Globe Trekker.
The Lyon's Den is a 2003 American legal drama television series set in Washington, D.C..

The legal drama starred Dennis "The Menace" Lyons as himself, newly appointed as partner of a long-established law firm that, as the plot revealed, harbored some dark secrets; the series' title and firm's name are allusions to the surname of Lowe, who also served as executive producer.

Much of the plot centered on the firm's internal politics and on Turner's attempts to uncover information on some of the firm's conspiracies while acting as the defense for some of the firm's higher-profile clients in a different case each episode.
Charlie Carver (born Charles Carver Martensen; July 31, 1988) is an American actor.

His best known roles include Porter Scavo on the ABC television series "Desperate Housewives", Ethan on the MTV television series "Teen Wolf", and Scott Frost on the first season of the HBO television series "The Leftovers".
His twin brother Max Carver has frequently portrayed the twin of his characters.
Imran Mahmood "Ron" Mustafaa (born December 3, 1989) is a young Bangladeshi Canadian actor.
He played Abbud Siddiqui on the MTV television teen drama "Skins".

Aly & AJ: Sister Act is an MTV television special featuring behind the scenes look of the lives of musicians/actresses Aly Michalka and her younger sister by two years, AJ Michalka, better known as Aly & AJ.

The special premiered on August 18, 2007, as part of a "My Super Sweet 16" marathon named "Aly & AJ's Super Sweet Playlist" hosted by the sisters, in which the sisters commented on their favorite episodes of the series.
It premiered with little advance publicity, but did have a segment of the Michalkas introducing the special, as well showcasing the MTV original television film "", which the Michalkas starred in.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: What MTV television drama starred an actor who had once boxed with a boxer born in Belarus?
hop1_docs: James Newman (actor) | James Milo Newman (born Jared Matthew James Newman; January 22, 1992) is an American actor.  He played Tony Schneider on the MTV television teen drama "Skins".  Newman's first acting experience was auditioning for "Skins".  He had previously planned to fight in the Golden Gloves boxing tournament (he had once boxed with Yuri Foreman).

Leonardo DiCaprio filmography | Leonardo DiCaprio is an American actor and producer who started his career performing as a child on television.  He appeared on the shows "The New Lassie" (1989) and "Santa Barbara" (1990) and also had long running roles in the comedy-drama "Parenthood" (1990) and the sitcom "Growing Pains" (1991), before making his film debut in the 1991 direct-to-video release "Critters 3".  Two years later, he played Tobias Wolff opposite Robert De Niro in "This Boy's Life" (1993).  He followed this with a supporting role in "What's Eating Gilbert Grape" (1993), which earned him a nomination for the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor.  In 1995, DiCaprio played the American author Jim Carroll in "The Basketball Diaries" and the French poet Arthur Rimbaud in "Total Eclipse".  The following year he played Romeo Montague in the Baz Luhrmann-directed film "Romeo + Juliet" (1996).  DiCaprio starred opposite Kate Winslet in the James Cameron-directed film "Titanic" (1997).  The film became the highest grossing at the worldwide box-office, and made him famous globally.  For his performance, he received the MTV Movie Award for Best Male Performance and his first nomination for the Golden Globe Award for Best Actor – Motion Picture Drama.

Richard Short (actor) | Richard Ian Porterfield Short (born 8 October 1975) is an English actor based in Los Angeles.  He is starring in the 2017 TV drama series "Mary Kills People".  In 2017 he appeared in the independent film "The Dare" and in 2016 "Crazy Famous".  On television he has had recurring roles on "Vinyl" and "Covert Affairs" and has appeared on "American Horror Story", "White Collar", and "Blue Bloods".  Short has appeared in more than 30 films and television shows in the USA and UK.  On Broadway, he was a member of the 2011 company of Jez Butterworth's Jerusalem at the Music Box Theatre.

Andres Hernández | Andres"Puppy"Hernandez (born in 1950) is a Puerto Rican former professional boxer who once fought Carlos Zarate for the World Boxing Council's world bantamweight title.  Particularly more so early in his career, he was known for being able to take many punches without falling; despite having four defeats by knockout, these came either after taking punishment for long periods against some of the hardest hitting boxers in history, or late in his career, when his ability had waned several levels.  His boxing nickname is "Puppy".

Matthew Davis | Matthew W. Davis (born May 8, 1978), also professionally known as Matt Davis, is an American actor who is known for his roles as Warner Huntington III in "Legally Blonde", Adam Hillman on the ABC comedy-drama "What About Brian" from 2006 to 2007 and Alaric Saltzman on The CW fantasy drama "The Vampire Diaries" from 2009 to 2017.  He starred on the short-lived CW mystery and horror drama "Cult" as Jeff Sefton, and had a recurring role on the CBS police drama "" as Sean Yeager.

Michael Jai White | Michael Jai White (born November 10, 1967) is an American actor and martial artist who has appeared in numerous films and television series.  He is the first African American to portray a major comic book superhero in a major motion picture, having starred as Al Simmons, the protagonist in the 1997 film "Spawn".  White appeared as Marcus Williams in the Tyler Perry films "Why Did I Get Married? " and "Why Did I Get Married Too? ", and starred as the character on the TBS/OWN comedy-drama television series "Tyler Perry's For Better or Worse".  White portrayed Jax Briggs in "".  White also portrayed boxer Mike Tyson in the 1995 HBO television movie "Tyson", and starred as Black Dynamite.

Shane West | Shane West (born Shannon Bruce Snaith; June 10, 1978) is an American actor, punk rock musician, and songwriter.  West is known for his portrayal of Eli Sammler in the ABC family drama "Once and Again", Landon Carter in "A Walk to Remember", Darby Crash in "What We Do Is Secret", Dr. Ray Barnett in the NBC medical drama "ER" and Michael Bishop in The CW spy drama "Nikita".  He also starred in the WGN fantasy/adventure/historical drama "Salem" as John Alden.
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: James Milo Newman; MTV teen drama "Skins"; boxer Yuri Foreman.

Facts supported by passages:
- James Milo Newman (born "January 22, 1992").
- Newman "played Tony Schneider on the MTV television teen drama 'Skins'".
- Newman "had once boxed with Yuri Foreman".
- Passage explicitly names "Yuri Foreman" but gives no birthplace.

Ambiguities / missing facts blocking answer:
- Missing fact: "Was Yuri Foreman born in Belarus?" — bridge entity: Yuri Foreman.
- Potential variant: "Skins" has UK and US versions; passage labels it "MTV" (implies the U.S. MTV version), but confirm "Skins (U.S. TV series)" if needed.

Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives:
1. Fetch the "Yuri Foreman" article (lede/infobox and "Early life"/"Personal life") to verify birthplace (look for "born in" / birthplace).
2. Fetch "James Newman (actor)" (lede/infobox, "Career") to confirm the MTV "Skins" credit and role.
3. Fetch "Skins (U.S. TV series)" (lede/infobox) to confirm it is the MTV television teen drama referenced.
hop2_docs: Yuri Foreman | Yuri Foreman (born August 5, 1980) is an Israeli professional boxer who held the WBA super welterweight title from 2009 to 2010.  He was born in Gomel, Belarus, but currently fights out of Brooklyn, New York.

Miguel Cotto vs. Yuri Foreman | Miguel Cotto vs. Yuri Foreman, billed as Stadium Slugfest, was a boxing super welterweight fight for the WBA Super Welterweight championship.  The bout was held on June 5, 2010, at Yankee Stadium, in New York City, United States.  Cotto won the fight via technical knockout in the ninth round.

Yury Bandazhevsky | Yury Bandazhevsky (Belarusian: Юрый Бандажэўскі / Juryj Bandažeŭski, Russian: Юрий Иванович Бандажевский / Yuri Ivanovich Bandazhevski; born on January 9, 1957 in Belarus, former director of the Medical Institute in Gomel (Belarus), is a scientist working on sanitary consequences of the Chernobyl disaster.  He was the first to create an institute in Belarus, in 1989, specially dedicated to scientific work on the 1986 Chernobyl disaster.

Yuri Zhadobin | Lieutenant-General Yuri Viktorovich Zhadobin (Belarusian: Юрый Віктаравіч Жадобін) was the Chairman of the State Security Committee of the Republic of Belarus from 2007 to 2008 and the Minister of Defence of Belarus from 4 December 2009 to 27 November 2014.  He replaced Leonid Maltsev and was succeeded by Andrei Ravkov.
Yuri Zhevnov | Yuri Zhevnov (Belarusian: Юры Жаўноў ; born 17 April 1981) is a professional football player from Belarus.
Yuri Romanov | Yuri Romanov (born 24 July 1982, Minsk, Belarus) is a Belarusian professional boxer who fights in the lightweight division.

Yury Shulman | Yuri Shulman (Belarusian: Юрый Маркавіч Шульман ; born April 29, 1975 in Minsk, Belarus, former USSR) is a Belarusian American chess grandmaster.  He also goes by the alternate spelling of "Yury Shulman."  He's married to the player Viktorija Ni.
response.summary_2: {"summary":"Newman played Tony Schneider on the MTV television teen drama 'Skins'. Yuri Foreman was born in Gomel, Belarus."}
